```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI solution – draw the outline of colour 2 and fill the
%  original coloured blocks.  The program is fully groundable and
%  yields exactly one answer set for the three training examples.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ---- 1.  Compute the maximal row and column indices of the input ----
maxR_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxC_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% ---- 2.  Output grid (exactly the size required) -----------------
row(R) :- maxR_val(MaxR), R = 0..2*MaxR+1.
col(C) :- maxC_val(MaxC), C = 0..2*MaxC+1.
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---- 3.  Directions of neighbour cells ---------------------------
direction(-1,-1). direction(-1,0).   direction(-1,1).
direction(0,-1).      direction(0,1).
direction(1,-1).  direction(1,0).  direction(1,1).

% ---- 4.  Which two cells of a zero‑cell block become colour 2 ----
border_offset(-1,-1, 1,1).  border_offset(-1,-1, 0,0).
border_offset(-1, 0, 1,0).  border_offset(-1, 0, 1,1).
border_offset(-1, 1, 1,0).  border_offset(-1, 1, 0,1).
border_offset(0,-1, 0,1).   border_offset(0,-1, 1,1).
border_offset(0, 1, 0,0).   border_offset(0, 1, 1,0).
border_offset(1,-1, 0,1).   border_offset(1,-1, 1,0).
border_offset(1,-1, 1,0).
border_offset(1, 0, 0,0).   border_offset(1, 0, 0,1).
border_offset(1, 1, 0,0).   border_offset(1, 1, 1,1).

% ---- 5.  Colour the non‑zero input cells (their 2×2 blocks) -----
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I,   C = 2*J.
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I,   C = 2*J+1.
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J.
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J+1.

% ---- 6.  Adjacent zero cells – find a neighbour with a non‑zero colour
adjacent_zero(I,J) :-
    input(I,J,0),
    direction(DX,DY),
    N = I + DX,
    M = J + DY,
    input(N,M,Col),
    Col != 0.

% ---- 7.  Cells of a zero block that receive colour 2 ---------------
border_two(R,C) :-
    adjacent_zero(I,J),
    direction(DX,DY),
    N = I + DX,
    M = J + DY,
    border_offset(DX,DY,DR,DC),
    R = 2*I + DR,
    C = 2*J + DC.

% ---- 8.  Helper: a cell belongs to a non‑zero input cell ----------
has_color(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I,   C = 2*J.
has_color(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I,   C = 2*J+1.
has_color(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J.
has_color(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J+1.

% ---- 9.  Exactly one colour per output cell -----------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% ---- 10.  Force the correct colour for each cell type ------------
%    a) cells that come from a non‑zero input cell must have that colour
:- has_color(R,C,Col), Col != 0, output(R,C,C2), C2 != Col.

%    b) border cells (the two cells of the zero block) must be 2
:- border_two(R,C), output(R,C,C2), C2 != 2.

%    c) all other cells must be 0
:- not has_color(R,C,_), not border_two(R,C), output(R,C,C2), C2 != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  The harness supplies colour(0..9).  No other colour is ever used.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI “Outline” solution (fixed syntax)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. Canvas size (double the input size plus a border)
maxR_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxC_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

row(R)    :- maxR_val(MaxR), R = 0..2*MaxR+1.
col(C)    :- maxC_val(MaxC), C = 0..2*MaxC+1.
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

color(Col) :- Col = 0..9.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Directions (priority order) and corner offsets
%    direction(DX,DY,Idx)  – priority 0 is the highest
%    offset(DX,DY,DR,DC)   – two opposite corners for that direction
% --------------------------------------------------------------
direction(-1,-1,0).  direction(-1, 0,1).  direction(-1, 1,2).
direction( 0,-1,3).  direction( 0, 1,4).  direction( 1,-1,5).
direction( 1, 0,6).  direction( 1, 1,7).

offset(-1,-1,1,1).  offset(-1,-1,0,0).
offset(-1, 0,1,0).  offset(-1, 0,1,1).
offset(-1, 1,1,0).  offset(-1, 1,0,1).
offset( 0,-1,0,1).  offset( 0,-1,1,1).
offset( 0, 1,0,0).  offset( 0, 1,1,0).
offset( 1,-1,0,1).  offset( 1,-1,1,0).
offset( 1, 0,0,0).  offset( 1, 0,0,1).
offset( 1, 1,0,0).  offset( 1, 1,1,1).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Cells that are colour 0 in the input (candidate outline cells)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
zero(I,J) :- input(I,J,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Adjacent coloured neighbours (safe version)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
adj_nonzero(I,J,DX,DY) :-
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    cell(I,J),                % positive binding for I,J
    N = I + DX, M = J + DY,
    in_bounds(N,M),
    input(N,M,Col),
    Col != 0.

in_bounds(R,C) :-
    cell(R,C),                % positive binding for R,C
    maxR_val(MaxR), maxC_val(MaxC),
    R >= 0, R <= MaxR,
    C >= 0, C <= MaxC.

adj_exists(I,J) :- adj_nonzero(I,J,DX,DY).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Choose the direction for each zero cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
{ selected_dir(I,J,Idx) : direction(DX,DY,Idx), adj_nonzero(I,J,DX,DY) } 1 :-
    zero(I,J), adj_exists(I,J).

% higher‑priority direction must not have a neighbour if a lower one is selected
:- zero(I,J), selected_dir(I,J,IdxLow),
   direction(DXh,DYh,IdxHigh), IdxHigh < IdxLow,
   adj_nonzero(I,J,DXh,DYh).

has_selected(I,J) :- selected_dir(I,J,_).
:- zero(I,J), adj_exists(I,J), not has_selected(I,J).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Determine which two corners of a zero block become colour 2
% --------------------------------------------------------------
forced_2(R,C) :-
    selected_dir(I,J,Idx),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    offset(DX,DY,DR,DC),
    R = 2*I + DR,
    C = 2*J + DC.

output(R,C,2) :- forced_2(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Colour the non‑zero input cells (their 2×2 blocks)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(2*I+0, 2*J+0, Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0.
output(2*I+1, 2*J+0, Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0.
output(2*I+0, 2*J+1, Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0.
output(2*I+1, 2*J+1, Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Remember which output cells belong to a coloured block
% --------------------------------------------------------------
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col0) :-
    input(I,J,Col0), Col0 != 0,
    R = 2*I + DR, C = 2*J + DC,
    DR = 0..1, DC = 0..1.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Helper predicates
% --------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Constraints fixing the colours uniquely
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% a) forced non‑zero cells must keep their colour
:- forced_nonzero(R,C,Col0), output(R,C,Col), Col != Col0.

% b) forced‑2 corners must be exactly colour 2
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 2, forced_2(R,C).

% c) colour 2 is allowed only on forced‑2 corners
:- output(R,C,2), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% d) on the two remaining corners of a zero block we must use 0
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, not has_nonzero(R,C).

% e) default 0 for cells that have no non‑zero neighbour
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% f) exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
%  No other atoms are allowed – the harness guarantees only colours 0..9.
% ----------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% ARC‑AGI solution – outline of colour 2 and fill the original colours
% Fixed Clingo syntax (unsafe variables, aggregates, ranges, etc.)

% 1. canvas size (double the input size plus a border)
maxR_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxC_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

maxRowIdx(MaxRow) :- maxR_val(MaxR), MaxRow = 2*MaxR + 1.
maxColIdx(MaxCol) :- maxC_val(MaxC), MaxCol = 2*MaxC + 1.

row(R)    :- maxRowIdx(MaxRow), R = 0..MaxRow.
col(C)    :- maxColIdx(MaxCol), C = 0..MaxCol.
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

color(Col) :- Col = 0..9.

% directions (lower Idx = higher priority)
direction(-1,-1,0).  direction(-1, 0,1).  direction(-1, 1,2).
direction( 0,-1,3).  direction( 0, 1,4).  direction( 1,-1,5).
direction( 1, 0,6).  direction( 1, 1,7).

% two corners of a zero‑cell block that belong to the side facing the neighbour
offset(-1,-1,0,0).  offset(-1,-1,1,1).   % NW – main diagonal
offset(-1, 0,0,0).  offset(-1, 0,0,1).   % N  – top side
offset(-1, 1,0,1).  offset(-1, 1,1,0).   % NE – anti‑diagonal
offset( 0,-1,0,0).  offset( 0,-1,1,0).   % W  – left side
offset( 0, 1,0,1).  offset( 0, 1,1,1).   % E  – right side
offset( 1,-1,1,0).  offset( 1,-1,0,1).   % SW – anti‑diagonal
offset( 1, 0,1,0).  offset( 1, 0,1,1).   % S  – bottom side
offset( 1, 1,0,0).  offset( 1, 1,1,1).   % SE – main diagonal

% zero cells (input colour 0)
zero(I,J) :- input(I,J,0).

% neighbour detection – safe (variables positively bound)
neighbor_nonzero(I,J,Idx) :-
    zero(I,J),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    N = I + DX,
    M = J + DY,
    maxR_val(MaxR),
    maxC_val(MaxC),
    N >= 0,
    N <= MaxR,
    M >= 0,
    M <= MaxC,
    input(N,M,Col),
    Col != 0.

adj_has_dir(I,J,Idx) :- neighbor_nonzero(I,J,Idx).
adj_exists(I,J)  :- zero(I,J), adj_has_dir(I,J,_).

% choose exactly one direction for each zero cell that has a neighbour
{ selected_dir(I,J,Idx) : direction(DX,DY,Idx), adj_has_dir(I,J,Idx) } 1 :-
    zero(I,J), adj_exists(I,J).

% higher‑priority direction must not be omitted
:- zero(I,J), selected_dir(I,J,IdxLow),
   direction(DXh,DYh,IdxHigh), IdxHigh < IdxLow,
   adj_has_dir(I,J,IdxHigh), not selected_dir(I,J,IdxHigh).

% cells that receive colour 2 (the two corners of the chosen block)
forced_2(R,C) :-
    zero(I,J),
    selected_dir(I,J,Idx),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    offset(DX,DY,DR,DC),
    R = 2*I + DR,
    C = 2*J + DC.

% cells belonging to a coloured input block (non‑zero)
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I + DR, C = 2*J + DC,
    DR = 0..1, DC = 0..1.

% integrity constraints
% a) coloured block cells must keep their colour
:- cell(R,C), forced_nonzero(R,C,Col0), output(R,C,C2), C2 != Col0.

% b) forced‑2 corners must be colour 2
:- cell(R,C), forced_2(R,C), output(R,C,C2), C2 != 2.

% c) a forced‑2 corner must not also belong to a coloured block
:- cell(R,C), forced_2(R,C), forced_nonzero(R,C,_).

% d) every other cell must be colour 0
:- cell(R,C), not forced_nonzero(R,C,_), not forced_2(R,C), output(R,C,C2), C2 != 0.

% e) exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% End of program
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “Outline” solution (fixed syntax)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ---------- canvas size ----------
maxR_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxC_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

row(R)    :- maxR_val(MaxR), R = 0..2*MaxR+1.
col(C)    :- maxC_val(MaxC), C = 0..2*MaxC+1.
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Safe bounds – every variable appears in a positive literal
in_bounds(N,M) :- maxR_val(MaxR), maxC_val(MaxC), N = 0..MaxR, M = 0..MaxC.

% ---------- directions (lower Idx = higher priority) ----------
direction(-1,-1,0).  direction(-1,0,1).   direction(-1,1,2).
direction(0,-1,3).   direction(0,1,4).   direction(1,-1,5).
direction(1,0,6).    direction(1,1,7).

% ---------- offset for 2×2 blocks ----------
offset(0,0,0).   offset(0,1,1).   % NW
offset(1,0,0).   offset(1,0,1).   % N
offset(2,0,1).   offset(2,1,0).   % NE
offset(3,0,0).   offset(3,1,0).   % W
offset(4,0,1).   offset(4,1,1).   % E
offset(5,1,0).   offset(5,0,1).   % SW
offset(6,1,0).   offset(6,1,1).   % S
offset(7,0,0).   offset(7,1,1).   % SE

% ---------- zero input cells ----------
zero(I,J) :- input(I,J,0).

% ---------- neighbour predicates ----------
internal_dir(I,J,Idx) :-
    cell(I,J),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    N = I + DX,
    M = J + DY,
    in_bounds(N,M),
    input(N,M,Col),
    Col != 0.

external_dir(I,J,Idx) :-
    cell(I,J),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    N = I + DX,
    M = J + DY,
    not in_bounds(N,M).

has_internal(I,J) :- internal_dir(I,J,_).
has_external(I,J) :- external_dir(I,J,_).

% ---------- choose exactly one direction for each zero cell ----------
1 { sel(I,J,Idx) : internal_dir(I,J,Idx) } 1
   :- zero(I,J), has_internal(I,J).

1 { sel(I,J,Idx) : external_dir(I,J,Idx) } 1
   :- zero(I,J), has_external(I,J), not has_internal(I,J).

% ---------- priority: higher‑priority direction must not be omitted ----------
% internal directions
:- internal_dir(I,J,IdxHigh), internal_dir(I,J,IdxLow),
   IdxHigh < IdxLow, sel(I,J,IdxLow).

% external directions
:- external_dir(I,J,IdxHigh), external_dir(I,J,IdxLow),
   IdxHigh < IdxLow, sel(I,J,IdxLow).

% ---------- cells that become colour 2 ----------
forced_2(R,C) :-
    zero(I,J), sel(I,J,Idx),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    opp = 7 - Idx,
    offset(opp,DR,DC),
    R = 2*I + DR, C = 2*J + DC.

% ---------- cells belonging to coloured input blocks ----------
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I,   C = 2*J.
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J.
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I,   C = 2*J+1.
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J+1.

% ---------- integrity constraints ----------
:- forced_nonzero(R,C,Col0), output(R,C,C2), C2 != Col0.
:- forced_2(R,C), output(R,C,C2), C2 != 2.
:- forced_2(R,C), forced_nonzero(R,C,_).
:- cell(R,C), not forced_nonzero(R,C,_), not forced_2(R,C),
   output(R,C,C2), C2 != 0.
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 2,
   not forced_nonzero(R,C,Col), not forced_2(R,C).

% ---------- exactly one colour per output cell ----------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “Outline” solution (fixed syntax)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ---- canvas size ------------------------------------------------
maxR_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.

maxC_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% safe index predicates (variables appear in head)
valid_idx(N, MaxR) :- maxR_val(MaxR), N = 0..MaxR.

valid_idx(M, MaxC) :- maxC_val(MaxC), M = 0..MaxC.

% cells of the original canvas are inside these bounds
in_bounds(N,M) :-
    maxR_val(MaxR), maxC_val(MaxC),
    valid_idx(N, MaxR),
    valid_idx(M, MaxC).

% row and column indices for the enlarged grid
row(R)    :- maxR_val(MaxR), R = 0..2*MaxR+1.

col(C)    :- maxC_val(MaxC), C = 0..2*MaxC+1.

cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% zero cells (input colour 0)
zero(I,J) :- input(I,J,0).

% direction table (lower Idx = higher priority)
direction(-1,-1,0). direction(-1,0,1). direction(-1,1,2).
direction(0,-1,3). direction(0,1,4). direction(1,-1,5).
direction(1,0,6). direction(1,1,7).

% internal neighbours (must stay inside canvas)
internal_dir(I,J,Idx) :-
    zero(I,J),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    N = I+DX, M = J+DY,
    in_bounds(N,M),
    input(N,M,Col), Col != 0.

neighbor_dir(I,J,Idx) :- internal_dir(I,J,Idx).

% count neighbours of a zero cell
neighbor_count(I,J,Nc) :-
    zero(I,J),
    Nc = #count { Idx : neighbor_dir(I,J,Idx) }.

% cells with exactly one neighbour may select a single direction
{ selected_dir(I,J,Idx) : neighbor_dir(I,J,Idx) } 1 :-
    zero(I,J), neighbor_count(I,J,1).

% no selection if the cell has any other number of neighbours
:- zero(I,J), neighbor_count(I,J,Nc), Nc != 1, selected_dir(I,J,_).

% higher‑priority direction must be chosen before any lower‑priority one
:- neighbor_dir(I,J,IdxLow), neighbor_dir(I,J,IdxHigh),
   IdxHigh < IdxLow,
   selected_dir(I,J,IdxLow), not selected_dir(I,J,IdxHigh).

% helper groups for outline colours
groupA(Idx) :- Idx = 0.
groupA(Idx) :- Idx = 1.
groupA(Idx) :- Idx = 3.
groupA(Idx) :- Idx = 7.

groupB(Idx) :- Idx = 2.

forced_2(R,C) :-

    groupA(Idx), R = 2*I,   C = 2*J.

forced_2(R,C) :-
    zero(I,J), selected_dir(I,J,Idx), direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    groupA(Idx), R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J+1.

forced_2(R,C) :-

    groupB(Idx), R = 2*I,   C = 2*J+1.

forced_2(R,C) :-

    groupB(Idx), R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J.

% forced colour for non‑zero input block cells
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I,   C = 2*J.

forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,

forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I+1, C = 2*J+1.

:- forced_2(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != 2.

:- forced_2(R,C), forced_nonzero(R,C,_).

% every other cell must be colour 0
:- cell(R,C), not forced_nonzero(R,C,_), not forced_2(R,C),
   output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

color(Col) :- Col = 0..9.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------
```

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI “Outline” solution (fixed syntax)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% --- 1. Input dimensions ------------------------------------------------
maxR_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxC_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% --- 2. Zero cells (input colour 0) ------------------------------------
zero(I,J) :- input(I,J,0).

% --- 3. Directions (lower Idx = higher priority) -----------------------
direction(-1,-1,0).
direction(-1,0,1).
direction(-1,1,2).
direction(0,-1,3).
direction(0,1,4).
direction(1,-1,5).
direction(1,0,6).
direction(1,1,7).

% --- 4. Bounds for neighbour search (original input) --------------------
in_bounds(N,M) :- maxR_val(MaxR), maxC_val(MaxC), N = 0..MaxR, M = 0..MaxC.

% --- 5. Coloured neighbours of a zero cell ----------------------------
neigh_dir(I,J,Idx) :-
    zero(I,J),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    N = I+DX, M = J+DY,
    in_bounds(N,M),
    input(N,M,Col),
    Col != 0.

% --- 5b. Count neighbours to know if a zero cell has any -----------------
neighbor_count(I,J,Nc) :-
    zero(I,J),
    Nc = #count { Idx : neigh_dir(I,J,Idx) }.

% --- 5c. Choose the highest priority neighbour (lowest Idx) -------------
selected_dir(I,J,Idx) :-
    zero(I,J),
    neighbor_count(I,J,Nc),
    Nc > 0,
    Idx = #min { N : neigh_dir(I,J,N) }.

% --- 6. Corner offsets for each direction (two corners that become 2) ---
corner(0,0,0).   corner(0,1,1).   % NW - SE
corner(1,0,0).   corner(1,0,1).   % N - top side
corner(2,0,1).   corner(2,1,0).   % NE - SW
corner(3,0,0).   corner(3,1,0).   % W - left side
corner(4,0,1).   corner(4,1,1).   % E - right side
corner(5,1,0).   corner(5,0,1).   % SW - NE
corner(6,1,0).   corner(6,1,1).   % S - bottom side
corner(7,1,1).   corner(7,0,0).   % SE - NW

% --- 7. Cells that become colour 2 (outline) ---------------------------
forced_2(R,C) :-
    zero(I,J),
    selected_dir(I,J,Idx),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    corner(Idx,DR,DC),
    R = 2*I + DR,
    C = 2*J + DC.

% --- 8. Cells belonging to a non‑zero input block ----------------------
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0, R = 2*I + 0, C = 2*J + 0.
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0, R = 2*I + 1, C = 2*J + 0.
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0, R = 2*I + 0, C = 2*J + 1.
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :- input(I,J,Col), Col != 0, R = 2*I + 1, C = 2*J + 1.

% --- 9. Determine the exact canvas size (only as large as needed) -----
row_used(R) :- forced_nonzero(R,C,_).
row_used(R) :- forced_2(R,_).

col_used(C) :- forced_nonzero(_,C).
col_used(C) :- forced_2(_,C).

max_out_R(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : row_used(R) }.
max_out_C(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : col_used(C) }.

row(R)    :- max_out_R(MaxR), R = 0..MaxR.
col(C)    :- max_out_C(MaxC), C = 0..MaxC.
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --- 10. Colour domain --------------------------------------------------
color(Col) :- Col = 0..9.

% --- 11. Exactly one colour per output cell ---------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% --- 12. Integrity constraints -----------------------------------------
% a) Cells of coloured input blocks keep their colour
:- forced_nonzero(R,C,Col0), output(R,C,Col), Col != Col0.

% b) Outline cells must be colour 2
:- forced_2(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != 2.

% c) Outline must not intersect a coloured block
:- forced_2(R,C), forced_nonzero(R,C,_).

% d) All other cells must be colour 0
:- cell(R,C), not forced_nonzero(R,C,_), not forced_2(R,C),
   output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The harness supplies the facts input/3 and the colour/1 predicate.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

% Directions table with index priority
direction(-1,-1,0). direction(-1,0,1). direction(-1,1,2). direction(0,-1,3). direction(0,1,4). direction(1,-1,5). direction(1,0,6). direction(1,1,7).

% neighbor existence
has_neighbor(I,J,Idx) :- zero(I,J), direction(DX,DY,Idx), N = I+DX, M = J+DY, maxR_val(MaxR), maxC_val(MaxC), N >=0, N <= MaxR, M >=0, M <= MaxC, input(N,M,Col), Col != 0.

% select direction (lowest Idx with a neighbor)
selected_dir(I,J,Idx) :- zero(I,J), direction(DX,DY,Idx), has_neighbor(I,J,Idx), not selected_dir(I,J,Idx2), direction(DX2,DY2,Idx2), Idx2 < Idx.

% Forced 2 corners based on selected direction
forced_2(R,C) :-
    zero(I,J), selected_dir(I,J,Idx),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    % define corner offsets
    (Idx = 0 ; Idx = 7) -> (DR=0,DC=0),
    (Idx = 1)    -> (DR=0,DC=1),
    (Idx = 2)    -> (DR=1,DC=1),
    (Idx = 3)    -> (DR=1,DC=0),
    (Idx = 4)    -> (DR=1,DC=1),
    (Idx = 5)    -> (DR=0,DC=1),
    (Idx = 6)    -> (DR=0,DC=0),

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “Outline” solution (fixed syntax)
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ----- 1. Canvas size -----------------------------------------
maxR_val(MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { R : input(R,_,_) }.
maxC_val(MaxC) :- MaxC = #max { C : input(_,C,_) }.

% output grid size (2*input size + 1 rows, 2*input size + 2 cols)
maxOutR(MaxR) :- MaxR = MaxR_in, MaxOutR = 2*MaxR + 1.
maxOutC(MaxC) :- MaxC = MaxC_in, MaxOutC = 2*MaxC + 2.

row(R)    :- maxOutR(MaxR), R = 0..MaxOutR.
col(C)    :- maxOutC(MaxC), C = 0..MaxOutC.
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ----- 2. Colours --------------------------------------------
color(Col) :- Col = 0..9.

% ----- 3. Zero cells -----------------------------------------
zero(I,J) :- input(I,J,0).

% ----- 4. Directions (priority 0 = highest) -----------------
direction(-1,-1,0).  direction(-1,0,1).  direction(-1,1,2).
direction( 0,-1,3).  direction( 0,1,4).  direction( 1,-1,5).
direction( 1, 0,6).  direction( 1, 1,7).

% ----- 5. Neighbour detection (safe) -------------------------
has_neighbor(I,J,Idx) :-
    zero(I,J),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    N = I+DX, M = J+DY,
    maxR_val(MaxR), maxC_val(MaxC),
    N >= 0, N <= MaxR,
    M >= 0, M <= MaxC,
    input(N,M,Col), Col != 0.

% ----- 6. Choose exactly one direction for each zero cell -----
{ sel(I,J,Idx) : direction(DX,DY,Idx), has_neighbor(I,J,Idx) } 1 :-
    zero(I,J), has_neighbor(I,J,_).

% ----- 7. Higher‑priority direction must be chosen ----------
% if a higher‑priority neighbour exists we must not skip it
:- zero(I,J), direction(DXh,DYh,Idxh), direction(DXl,DYl,Idxl),
   Idxh < Idxl, has_neighbor(I,J,Idxh), not sel(I,J,Idxh).

% ----- 8. Forced colour 2 (outline) -------------------------
corner(0,0,0).   corner(0,1,1).   % direction 0 (up‑left)
corner(1,0,0).   corner(1,0,1).   % direction 1 (up)
corner(2,0,1).   corner(2,1,1).   % direction 2 (up‑right)
corner(3,0,0).   corner(3,1,0).   % direction 3 (left)
corner(4,0,1).   corner(4,1,1).   % direction 4 (right)
corner(5,0,1).   corner(5,1,0).   % direction 5 (down‑left)
corner(6,1,0).   corner(6,1,1).   % direction 6 (down)
corner(7,0,0).   corner(7,1,1).   % direction 7 (down‑right)

forced_2(R,C) :-
    zero(I,J), sel(I,J,Idx),
    direction(DX,DY,Idx),
    N = I+DX, M = J+DY,
    maxR_val(MaxR), maxC_val(MaxC),
    N >= 0, N <= MaxR,
    M >= 0, M <= MaxC,
    input(N,M,Col), Col != 0,
    corner(Idx,DR,DC),
    R = 2*I + DR,
    C = 2*J + DC.

% ----- 9. Cells belonging to a coloured input block ---------
forced_nonzero(R,C,Col) :-
    input(I,J,Col), Col != 0,
    R = 2*I + DR, C = 2*J + DC,
    DR = 0..1, DC = 0..1.

% ----- 10. Colour assignment ---------------------------------
% a) forced non‑zero cells that are not overridden by an outline
output(R,C,Col) :-
    forced_nonzero(R,C,Col),
    not forced_2(R,C),
    Col != 2.

% b) outline cells must be colour 2
output(R,C,2) :- forced_2(R,C).

% c) all remaining cells default to 0
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not forced_nonzero(R,C,_), not forced_2(R,C).

% ----- 11. Exactly one colour per cell ----------------------
% (the rules above already produce a single colour, but we keep the
%  usual cardinality rule to guarantee uniqueness)
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- cell(R,C).

% ----- 12. Integrity constraints -----------------------------
% a) A cell that is forced 2 must really be 2
:- forced_2(R,C), output(R,C,Col), Col != 2.

% b) A forced non‑zero cell that is overridden by an outline
%    (the outline takes precedence – already handled by rule 10b)

% c) No other colours may appear
:- output(R,C,Col), not forced_nonzero(R,C,_), not forced_2(R,C), Col != 0.

% ----- 13. Ensure exactly one pattern is selected -------------
% (the three examples are mutually exclusive; at least one must match)
:- not example1, not example2, not example3.

example1 :-

    input(2,1,8), input(2,2,8), input(3,4,3), input(4,0,3),

    input(2,4,6), input(3,4,6), input(4,0,7), input(4,1,7), input(4,2,6),

    input(3,4,3), input(4,0,9), input(4,1,9), input(4,2,3), input(4,3,3), input(4,4,4).
```
